overview: The Markdown Filter extracts translatable text from Markdown files based on the [CommonMark](http://commonmark.org) specification, with additional support for [GitHub-flavored Markdown](https://guides.github.com/features/mastering-markdown/). HTML elements within Markdown are processed by the HTML filter (configurable separately). The filter supports inline code finding, subfilter delegation for HTML and YAML content, and various options to control which Markdown constructs are exposed for translation.

translateUrls.help:
URLs in link (`[text](url)`) and image (`![alt](url)`) statements are normally treated as non-translatable. Enabling this extracts URLs as **subflows** — they appear as separate translation units *before* the segment that references them.

The extracted subflow is referenced from the main segment using `[#$tuN]` notation in the XLIFF output.
notes: When subflows are extracted, the subflow translation unit appears **before** the segment containing the link in the XLIFF file.

urlToTranslatePattern.help: Restricts which URLs are extracted for translation when `translateUrls` is enabled. Only URLs matching this Java regular expression will be extracted as subflows.
urlToTranslatePattern.values: Java regex pattern. Default `.+` matches all URLs.
examples: `^https://example\.com/.*` — only extract URLs from a specific domain
`.*\.(html|htm)$` — only extract URLs pointing to HTML pages

dependsOn.condition: must be true for this pattern to have any effect

translateCodeBlocks.help: Controls whether the contents of **fenced** code blocks (delimited by ` ``` ` or `~~~`) are exposed for translation. Fenced code blocks often contain code samples that should not be translated, but may contain user-facing text in documentation contexts.

translateIndentedCodeBlocks.help: Controls whether the contents of **indented** code blocks (lines indented by 4+ spaces or 1+ tab) are exposed for translation. These are an alternative code block syntax in CommonMark.

translateInlineCodeBlocks.help: Controls whether text delimited by **single backticks** (`` `inline code` ``) is exposed for translation. Inline code typically contains technical terms, commands, or variable names that may not need translation.

translateImageAltText.help: Extracts alt text from images for translation. Applies to both Markdown image syntax (`![alt text](url)`) and HTML `` tags with `alt` attributes (``).

generateHeaderAnchors.help:
Automatically generates explicit named anchors for headings using the `{#my-anchor}` syntax. This provides **stable anchors** for hyperlinks that reference translatable header text — without this, links to headers may break when the header text is translated.

Some Markdown parsers support explicit named anchors in header markup.
examples: `## My Section` → `## My Section {#my-section}` — anchor generated from header text

parseMdx.help: Parses out multi-line `export` blocks as skeleton (non-translatable). This is useful for MDX files that mix Markdown with JSX/JavaScript export expressions.
notes: **Experimental** — this feature uses regex-based parsing of MDX expressions and may not handle all edge cases.

htmlEntitesToEscape.help:
Specifies characters that should be encoded as HTML entities on export. Enter the raw characters (not the entity names) as a single string.

Leave empty to disable entity escaping.
htmlEntitesToEscape.values: A string of characters, e.g. `<>&` would escape those three characters to `<`, `>`, `&`.

unescapeBackslashCharacters.help:
Enables parsing of backslash-escaped punctuation in source documents. When enabled, the filter recognizes constructs like `\*` as escaped punctuation rather than literal backslash + character.

On export, characters listed in `charactersToEscape` will be re-escaped with backslashes.

charactersToEscape.help: Lists the punctuation characters that will be backslash-escaped on export. Only applies when `unescapeBackslashCharacters` is enabled.
charactersToEscape.values: A string of punctuation characters. Default: ``*_`{}[]<>()#+\-.!|``

dependsOn.condition: must be true for this setting to take effect

htmlSubfilter.help:
Specifies a custom HTML filter configuration to use when processing HTML elements within Markdown. The configuration file must be saved with a `.fprm` suffix in a known location.

Leave empty to use the default HTML filter configuration (which is tailored for Markdown content).

To customize inline code patterns for HTML content within Markdown, configure them in the HTML subfilter configuration, not in the Markdown filter's own inline code settings.
htmlSubfilter.values: Configuration ID in the format `okf_html@custom-name` (without `.fprm` suffix). The corresponding file `okf_html@custom-name.fprm` must exist.
notes: The Markdown filter's own Inline Code Finder does **not** apply to HTML inline tags or HTML blocks. To define inline codes for HTML content, configure them in the HTML subfilter.

yamlSubfilter.help:
Specifies a custom YAML filter configuration for processing any YAML metadata header detected in the document. This allows fine-grained control over which metadata fields are extracted for translation.

Leave empty to use the default YAML filter configuration.
yamlSubfilter.values: Configuration ID referencing a YAML filter configuration (`.fprm` file).

nonTranslateBlocks.help: Prevents specific block quotes from being extracted for translation. Block quotes whose content starts with one of the specified strings will be skipped entirely.
nonTranslateBlocks.values: Comma-separated list of prefix strings. Any block quote starting with one of these strings will not be extracted.
examples: `Note:,Warning:` — skip block quotes that start with 'Note:' or 'Warning:'

useCodeFinder.help:
Enables the Inline Code Finder, which uses regex patterns to identify inline codes within translatable text. Matched patterns are treated as non-translatable codes (placeholders) within segments.

This applies only to the Markdown portion of the document — for HTML content within Markdown, configure inline codes in the HTML subfilter separately.
notes: Inline Code Finder support was temporarily unavailable in some snapshot builds of version 0.36, but has been restored.

processingNotes: If the file has a Unicode Byte-Order-Mark (BOM), the corresponding encoding (UTF-8, UTF-16, etc.) is used automatically. Otherwise, the default encoding specified in the filter options is used.
HTML inline elements (tags) and HTML blocks (content between block-forming start/end tags) are delegated to the HTML filter for processing.
The Inline Code Finder applies only to translatable text in the Markdown portion of the document — it does **not** apply to HTML inline tags or HTML blocks.
URLs extracted for translation appear as **subflows** — separate translation units that are extracted *before* the segment referencing them.
limitations: Designed for CommonMark-based Markdown; not all Markdown dialects are fully supported.
MDX parsing (`parseMdx`) is experimental and uses regex-based extraction.

The wiki lists `translateImageAltText` as the parameter name for both 'Translate YAML Metadata Header' and 'Translate Image Alt Text' — this appears to be a documentation error; the YAML header parameter likely has a distinct internal name.

examples.title: URL translation as subflows
examples.description: When `translateUrls` is enabled, URLs in image/link statements are extracted as separate translation units (subflows) that appear before the referencing segment in the XLIFF output.
examples.input:
```markdown
Please click ![The Information desk logo](images/circled-i.jpg) for help.
```
examples.output:
```xml
<trans-unit id="tu2" restype="x-img-link" xml:space="preserve">
<source xml:lang="en">images/circled-i.jpg</source>
</trans-unit>
<trans-unit id="tu1" xml:space="preserve">
<source xml:lang="en">Please click <bpt id="1">![</bpt>The Information desk logo<ept id="1">]([#$tu2])</ept> for help.</source>
</trans-unit>
```

translateUrls.title: Translate Hyperlink URLs
translateUrls.description: Extract URLs in link and image statements as translatable subflows.

urlToTranslatePattern.title: Translatable URL Pattern
urlToTranslatePattern.description: Regex pattern to filter which URLs are extracted when URL translation is enabled.

translateCodeBlocks.title: Translate Fenced Code Blocks
translateCodeBlocks.description: Expose contents of fenced code blocks for translation.

translateIndentedCodeBlocks.title: Translate Indented Code Blocks
translateIndentedCodeBlocks.description: Expose contents of indented code blocks for translation.

translateInlineCodeBlocks.title: Translate Inline Code Blocks
translateInlineCodeBlocks.description: Expose contents of backtick-delimited inline code for translation.

translateImageAltText.title: Translate Image Alt Text
translateImageAltText.description: Extract image alt text from Markdown and HTML img tags for translation.

generateHeaderAnchors.title: Generate Header Anchors
generateHeaderAnchors.description: Automatically generate named anchors for headings to preserve link targets.

parseMdx.title: Parse MDX Expressions
parseMdx.description: Parse out multi-line MDX export blocks as non-translatable skeleton.

htmlEntitesToEscape.title: HTML Entities to Escape
htmlEntitesToEscape.description: Characters to encode as HTML entities on export.

unescapeBackslashCharacters.title: Support Backslash Escaping
unescapeBackslashCharacters.description: Parse backslash-escaped punctuation in source documents.

charactersToEscape.title: Characters to Escape
charactersToEscape.description: Punctuation characters to backslash-escape on export.

htmlSubfilter.title: HTML Subfilter Configuration
htmlSubfilter.description: Custom HTML filter configuration ID for processing HTML within Markdown.

yamlSubfilter.title: YAML Subfilter Configuration
yamlSubfilter.description: Custom YAML filter configuration ID for processing YAML metadata headers.

nonTranslateBlocks.title: Non-Translatable Block Quotes
nonTranslateBlocks.description: Comma-separated prefixes identifying block quotes to exclude from translation.

useCodeFinder.title: Use Code Finder
useCodeFinder.description: Enable the Inline Code Finder for identifying inline codes in translatable text.

fullDoc: # Markdown Filter The Markdown Filter extracts translatable text from Markdown files based on the [CommonMark](http://commonmark.org) specification, with additional support for [GitHub-flavored Markdown](https://guides.github.com/features/mastering-markdown/). HTML elements within Markdown are processed by the HTML filter (configurable separately). The filter supports inline code finding, subfilter delegation for HTML and YAML content, and various options to control which Markdown constructs are exposed for translation. ## Parameters ### URL Translation #### Translate Hyperlink URLs (`translateUrls`) URLs in link (`[text](url)`) and image (`![alt](url)`) statements are normally treated as non-translatable. Enabling this extracts URLs as **subflows** — they appear as separate translation units *before* the segment that references them. **Note:** When subflows are extracted, the subflow translation unit appears **before** the segment containing the link in the XLIFF file. #### Translatable URL Pattern (`urlToTranslatePattern`) Restricts which URLs are extracted for translation when `translateUrls` is enabled. Only URLs matching this Java regular expression will be extracted as subflows. Default: `.+` (all URLs). Only takes effect when `translateUrls` is true. ### Code Block Translation #### Translate Fenced Code Blocks (`translateCodeBlocks`) Controls whether the contents of fenced code blocks (delimited by ` ``` ` or `~~~`) are exposed for translation. Default: true. #### Translate Indented Code Blocks (`translateIndentedCodeBlocks`) Controls whether the contents of indented code blocks (lines indented by 4+ spaces or 1+ tab) are exposed for translation. Default: true. #### Translate Inline Code Blocks (`translateInlineCodeBlocks`) Controls whether text delimited by single backticks is exposed for translation. Default: true. ### Content Extraction #### Translate Image Alt Text (`translateImageAltText`) Extracts alt text from images for translation. Applies to both Markdown image syntax (`![alt text](url)`) and HTML `` tags with `alt` attributes. Default: true. #### Generate Header Anchors (`generateHeaderAnchors`) Automatically generates explicit named anchors for headings using the `{#my-anchor}` syntax. This provides stable anchors for hyperlinks that reference translatable header text. Default: false. #### Non-Translatable Block Quotes (`nonTranslateBlocks`) Prevents specific block quotes from being extracted for translation. Block quotes whose content starts with one of the specified comma-separated strings will be skipped entirely. Default: empty (all block quotes extracted). ### MDX Support #### Parse MDX Expressions (`parseMdx`) Parses out multi-line `export` blocks as skeleton (non-translatable). Useful for MDX files that mix Markdown with JSX/JavaScript. **Warning:** This feature is **experimental** and uses regex-based parsing. ### Character Escaping #### HTML Entities to Escape (`htmlEntitesToEscape`) Specifies characters that should be encoded as HTML entities on export. Enter the raw characters as a single string. Default: empty. #### Support Backslash Escaping (`unescapeBackslashCharacters`) Enables parsing of backslash-escaped punctuation in source documents. On export, characters listed in `charactersToEscape` will be re-escaped. Default: false. #### Characters to Escape (`charactersToEscape`) Lists the punctuation characters that will be backslash-escaped on export. Only applies when `unescapeBackslashCharacters` is enabled. Default: ``*_`{}[]<>()#+\-.!|`` ### Subfilters #### HTML Subfilter Configuration (`htmlSubfilter`) Custom HTML filter configuration ID for processing HTML elements within Markdown. The configuration file must be saved with a `.fprm` suffix. Leave empty for the default configuration tailored for Markdown. **Note:** The Markdown filter's own Inline Code Finder does not apply to HTML inline tags or HTML blocks — configure inline codes for HTML content in the HTML subfilter. #### YAML Subfilter Configuration (`yamlSubfilter`) Custom YAML filter configuration ID for processing YAML metadata headers. Allows fine-grained control over which metadata fields are extracted. Default: empty. ### Inline Code Finder #### Use Code Finder (`useCodeFinder`) Enables the Inline Code Finder, which uses regex patterns to identify inline codes within translatable text. Matched patterns are treated as non-translatable placeholders within segments. **Note:** This applies only to the Markdown portion — for HTML content within Markdown, configure inline codes in the HTML subfilter separately. Inline Code Finder support was temporarily unavailable in some snapshot builds of version 0.36 but has been restored. ## Limitations - Designed for CommonMark-based Markdown; not all Markdown dialects are fully supported. - MDX parsing (`parseMdx`) is experimental and uses regex-based extraction. - The wiki documents `translateImageAltText` as the parameter name for both the YAML metadata header and image alt text options — this appears to be a documentation error. ## Notes - If the file has a Unicode BOM, the corresponding encoding is used automatically; otherwise the default encoding from filter options is used. - HTML inline elements and HTML blocks within Markdown are delegated to the HTML filter for processing. - URLs extracted for translation appear as subflows — separate translation units extracted before the referencing segment. ## Examples ### URL Translation as Subflows When `translateUrls` is enabled, URLs in image/link statements are extracted as separate translation units: **Input:** ```markdown Please click ![The Information desk logo](images/circled-i.jpg) for help. ``` **Output (XLIFF):** ```xml images/circled-i.jpg Please click ![The Information desk logo]([#$tu2]) for help. ```
